coverage: Every reachable node type across all thirteen distinct Math Clay surface roots has a browser handler.
materialParity: The shared literal flat.w Background material renders as its authored unlit linear 0.8 gray. The Twisted Saddle's evaluated Filament and Cross Section material now reproduces the extracted Generated-coordinate 3D White Noise plus distorted Z Wave field, Rec.709 color conversion, derivative Bump relief, dynamic Principled roughness and coat fields, and the dark-HSV Screen/Window Wave backface emission branch; its exact Blender Eevee reference is dojo/references/math-clay/lorenze-authored.png. The Bevel For Eevee ambient-occlusion base normal, exact Blender coat IOR/Fresnel, toon outline, and npr glass remain separate parity targets.
validation: All thirteen roots evaluate with Blender's inclusive Volume Cube lattice, minimum-bound transform, sparse OpenVDB boundary guards, fixed ambiguous-cell edge groups, Blender-equivalent directional quad ordering, and float32 Stepped Map Range fields. Twelve full roots are topology-exact. Native Manifold face provenance reconstructs authored nonplanar polygons after hard-sphere cuts, and polygon-level cleanup removes intersection vertices that lie only on the input fan triangulation: lorenze.007 is exact at 16,249 / 15,459 and TPMS.018 is exact at 14,190 / 13,558, including Blender's complete face-size histograms. TPMS.016's full 74^3 Volume Cube field is value-exact against Blender. Reproducing copyFromDense's uniform 8^3 active-tile pruning and GridTransformer's float BoxSampler advances it to 87,095 / 85,386. Its deterministic 136,062 / 136,060 pre-Boolean mesh is a native OpenVDB 12 outcome: thirty identical native intersection-mask runs varied from 136,062 to 136,076 cells; twenty identical native mesh runs varied from 136,062 / 136,060 through 136,072 / 136,070 and produced the stored 136,066 / 136,064 reference three times. The four-count snapshot difference is therefore OpenVDB's parallel intersection-tree scheduling race, not a field, resampling, or browser topology mismatch.
